growth_records.strain: Natural microcosm enrichment (no single isolate); cedar bog, Falmouth, MA
growth_records.medium: Defined mineral salt medium (N-limited) with 4.9% methane-in-air gas feed

up.method: reported as CH4 consumption rate in Fig 2a (calculated from input-output gas concentrations x flow rate); numeric values shown only in figure, not stated in text, not normalized per gDW
reported as O2 consumption rate in Fig 2c (input-output gas x flow rate); numeric values only in figure, not in text
N-limiting substrate; feed 50-700 uM; residual near 0 uM when CH4 on (Fig 1a); no specific consumption rate stated

sec.method: reported as CO2 production rate in Fig 2b (input-output gas x flow rate); numeric values only in figure, not in text
accumulated during CH4-off periods, drawn to ~0 uM when CH4 on (Fig 1b); concentrations only, no flux

growth_records.cite: Fernandez-Gonzalez et al. Microbial Communities Are Well Adapted to Disturbances in Energy Input. mSystems (2016). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00117-16. [PMC5080406]
growth_records.snippet: the microcosms operated independently in chemostat mode with a defined mineral salt medium (70 uM K2HPO4, 700 uM KNO3, 100 uM MgSO4, 100 uM CaCl2, 100 uM NaCl) plus trace elements ... at dilution rate of 0.1 day-1 (1.25 ml.min-1).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state growth rate derived as mu = D. Stated D = 0.1 day-1 = 0.1/24 h = 0.004167 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.004167 = 166.4 h (=6.93 days). D confirmed by flow/volume: 1.25 ml/min x 1440 min/day = 1800 ml/day = 1.8 L/day; 1.8 L / 18 L = 0.1 day-1. Carbon/energy source = methane (4.9% in air); N source = nitrate (N-limited). Gas consumption/production rates (CH4, O2 uptake; CO2 output) are computed by the authors from input-output gas concentrations x flow rate but appear only graphically in Fig 2 with no numeric values in the main text and are not normalized to biomass (gDW), so specific fluxes (mmol/gDW/h) cannot be computed. Organism is a mixed methanotrophic enrichment community, not a single strain; value flagged low confidence. Temperature 20C stated. pH variable across phases; 6.5 buffer imposed in phase IV (control continuous-methane condition reported here). This mu=D applies to steady-state (control/continuous) operation; cycled-treatment chemostats had the same D but periodic 20-day CH4 on/off forcing (not strict steady state).
